what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard decision item that compares two ways to expose a git-tracked noticeboard folder as an Obsidian vault: pointing Obsidian directly at the folder (A1) versus mirroring the contents into a separate vault folder (A2). It evaluates the options across data freshness, git pollution, two-writer risk, mobile/iOS sync, enrichment safety, and effort.
observations: The item is structured as a two-column decision matrix for Axis A.
A1 drops .obsidian/ into the git-tracked company repo, which would require gitignore handling.
A2 keeps vault config out of the repo and is described as the natural fit for the mobile/iOS sync goal.
The author cites Tim's memory that the synced vault is Windows-side and WSL paths do not get mobile sync.
A one-way store-to-mirror sync is proposed to make the mirror a structurally-read-only lens.
L2 enrichment is mentioned as a concern: in A1 it mutates canonical files, in A2 it stays in the mirror.
The item links to parent item-4baaab55 via part_of.
The file has YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
standouts: Mobile/iOS sync is treated as the decisive use case for the project.
The mirror option is framed as a structurally-read-only lens that preserves host-not-master separation.
The .obsidian/ pollution risk in a git-tracked repo is flagged as observed.
concerns: The comparison is presented as neutral but the conclusion clearly favors A2 using evaluative language like natural fit and cleanest possible expression.
It relies on secondhand memory (Tim's note) as supporting evidence without a primary source.
No explicit final decision or next-step status is recorded.
notes: A design-decision noticeboard post weighing two vault layouts, where mobile sync and read-only enrichment safety are the decisive considerations.
questions: What exactly are L0, L1, and L2 enrichment in this context?
Which sync mechanism is intended: rsync, symlink, file watcher, or something else?
Has a final decision been ratified, or is this still a proposal?
What does host not master mean operationally for this system?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the cross-cutting Axis A decision; the actual content is a comparative table plus a recommendation, which matches the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Separation of data store from presentation tool
Read-only lenses and one-way data flow
intention_signals: The file appears to be making the case that a mirrored vault architecture is the right choice by showing that the mobile use case and enrichment safety justify the extra sync complexity.
novelty: It treats Obsidian vault placement as a cross-cutting architectural axis and explicitly frames the mirror not as a second copy but as a structurally-read-only lens.
contribution: It records the criteria and trade-offs for placing an Obsidian vault relative to a git-tracked noticeboard, preserving the mobile-sync rationale and the read-only-lens concept for later fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard decision item comparing two Obsidian vault layouts for a git-tracked channel-memory folder, evaluating freshness, git pollution, writer conflicts, mobile sync, enrichment safety, and effort.
intention_for_embedding: To document the Axis A cross-cutting decision and argue that a one-way mirrored vault best serves mobile sync and read-only enrichment safety.
